title: Such, Such Is Death by Charles Sorley — Inspirational Poetry Lines
content:
Such, such is Death: no triumph: no defeat:
Only an empty pail, a slate rubbed clean,
A merciful putting away of what has been.

And this we know: Death is not Life, effete,
Life crushed, the broken pail. We who have seen
So marvellous things know well the end not yet.
meaning: "Such, Such Is Death" by Charles Sorley is a inspirational poem consisting of 16 lines. The poet explores themes of inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Such, such is Death: no triumph: no defeat: Only an empty pail, a slate rubbed clean,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Charles Sorley's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
